nodes.name: Academy Awards
nodes.description: Oscar-winning films and landmark moments in Academy history

items.prompt: Which 1927 silent war film about World War I combat pilots was the first movie to win the Academy Award for Best Picture?
items.explanation:
Directed by William A. Wellman; produced by Lucien Hubbard for Paramount
Won Outstanding Picture at the 1st Academy Awards ceremony (May 16, 1929, Hollywood Roosevelt Hotel)
Stars Clara Bow, Charles 'Buddy' Rogers, and Richard Arlen as WWI fighter pilots
Only silent film ever to win Best Picture
Shot on location at Kelly Field in San Antonio, Texas, with actual Army planes and 3,500 extras
Budgeted at $2 million (roughly $35 million today), making it one of the most expensive films of its era
Features one of the earliest on-screen same-sex kisses in American cinema

items.prompt: Which 1939 Civil War epic starring Vivien Leigh and Clark Gable won eight competitive Oscars and featured the first African American Oscar winner?
items.explanation:
Directed by Victor Fleming; produced by David O. Selznick
Won eight competitive Oscars plus two honorary awards from 13 nominations at the 12th Academy Awards (February 29, 1940)
Hattie McDaniel became the first African American to win an Oscar (Best Supporting Actress) but was segregated at the ceremony
Based on Margaret Mitchell's 1936 novel; screenplay by Sidney Howard, who won posthumously (killed in a tractor accident)
Adjusted for inflation, remains the highest-grossing film of all time
Vivien Leigh beat 1,400 candidates for the role of Scarlett O'Hara in one of Hollywood's most publicized casting searches
The burning of Atlanta scene used old sets from *King Kong* and *The Garden of Allah*

items.prompt: Which 1942 wartime romance starring Humphrey Bogart and Ingrid Bergman, set in French Morocco, won Best Picture at the 1944 ceremony?
items.explanation:
Directed by Michael Curtiz; produced by Hal B. Wallis for Warner Bros.
Won Best Picture, Best Director, and Best Screenplay at the 16th Academy Awards (March 2, 1944)
Stars Humphrey Bogart as Rick Blaine and Ingrid Bergman as Ilsa Lund
Famous quotes include 'Here's looking at you, kid,' 'Of all the gin joints...,' and 'Play it, Sam' (often misquoted as 'Play it again, Sam')
The script was still being written during filming; actors often did not know the ending
Set and partially filmed during WWII; released weeks after the Allied invasion of North Africa, boosting its topicality
Consistently ranked among the top three on the AFI 100 Greatest American Films list

items.prompt: Which 1954 film starring Marlon Brando as a dockworker fighting union corruption features the famous line 'I coulda been a contender'?
items.explanation:
Directed by Elia Kazan; written by Budd Schulberg
Won eight Oscars from 12 nominations, including Best Picture, Best Director, Best Actor (Brando), and Best Supporting Actress (Eva Marie Saint)
Brando's 'I coulda been a contender' speech in the back of a taxi is one of the most iconic scenes in cinema
Brando played Terry Malloy, an ex-boxer who testifies against corrupt union boss Johnny Friendly
Widely read as Kazan's defense of his own testimony before the House Un-American Activities Committee in 1952
Shot on location on the docks of Hoboken, New Jersey, in the dead of winter
Eva Marie Saint's film debut; she went on to appear in Hitchcock's *North by Northwest* (1959)

items.prompt: Which 1961 musical, a modern retelling of *Romeo and Juliet* set among New York street gangs, won 10 of its 11 Oscar nominations?
items.explanation:
Co-directed by Robert Wise and Jerome Robbins; music by Leonard Bernstein, lyrics by Stephen Sondheim
Won 10 of 11 nominations at the 34th Academy Awards — the most wins for a musical ever
Rita Moreno won Best Supporting Actress, becoming the first Latina to win an Oscar
Wise and Robbins became the first co-directors to win Best Director
Adapts *Romeo and Juliet* to 1950s New York, with the Jets (white) and Sharks (Puerto Rican) replacing Montagues and Capulets
Bernstein's score includes 'Tonight,' 'America,' 'Somewhere,' and 'I Feel Pretty'
Steven Spielberg directed a 2021 remake; Moreno returned in a new role at age 89

items.prompt: Which 1972 gangster epic, directed by Francis Ford Coppola and starring Marlon Brando as Vito Corleone, won Best Picture?
items.explanation:
Directed by Francis Ford Coppola; based on Mario Puzo's 1969 novel
Won three Oscars: Best Picture, Best Actor (Brando), and Best Adapted Screenplay (Coppola and Puzo)
Brando refused his Best Actor Oscar; sent Sacheen Littlefeather to decline it in protest of Hollywood's treatment of Native Americans
Al Pacino's breakthrough as Michael Corleone; Paramount initially opposed his casting
The studio wanted a cheaper, shorter film; Coppola fought for the period setting, three-hour runtime, and Brando
'I'm gonna make him an offer he can't refuse' was voted the second-greatest movie quote by AFI
*The Godfather Part II* (1974) also won Best Picture — the only sequel to do so until *The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King* (2003)

items.prompt: Which 1975 film set in a psychiatric hospital, starring Jack Nicholson and directed by Milos Forman, swept the Big Five Oscar categories?
items.answer: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
alternates: One Flew Over the Cuckoos Nest
items.explanation:
Directed by Milos Forman; based on Ken Kesey's 1962 novel
Second film to win the 'Big Five' Oscars: Best Picture, Director, Actor (Nicholson), Actress (Louise Fletcher), and Screenplay
Only two other films have swept the Big Five: *It Happened One Night* (1934) and *The Silence of the Lambs* (1991)
Nicholson plays Randle McMurphy, a convict who fakes insanity and clashes with authoritarian Nurse Ratched
Filmed at Oregon State Hospital, an actual psychiatric institution, using real patients as extras
Produced by Michael Douglas and Saul Zaentz; Douglas bought the rights from his father Kirk, who starred in the 1963 Broadway version
Made $109 million on a $3 million budget
items.cardBack: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest

items.prompt: Which 1977 Woody Allen romantic comedy starring Diane Keaton beat *Star Wars* for Best Picture?
items.explanation:
Directed by Woody Allen; starring Allen and Diane Keaton
Won four Oscars: Best Picture, Best Director, Best Actress (Keaton), and Best Original Screenplay (Allen and Marshall Brickman)
Beat *Star Wars* for Best Picture, one of the most discussed upsets in Oscar history
Keaton's role inspired a fashion trend — the 'Annie Hall look' of men's vests, ties, and baggy trousers on women
Originally titled *Anhedonia* (the inability to feel pleasure) and was more of a surreal sketch comedy before editing
Allen did not attend the ceremony; he was playing clarinet at Michael's Pub in Manhattan, as he did every Monday night
Ranked #31 on AFI's 100 Greatest American Films and #4 on their greatest comedies list

items.prompt: Which 1993 Steven Spielberg film, shot in black and white, tells the story of a German industrialist who saved over 1,000 Jews during the Holocaust?
items.explanation:
Directed by Steven Spielberg; screenplay by Steven Zaillian, based on Thomas Keneally's novel *Schindler's Ark*
Won seven Oscars from 12 nominations, including Best Picture and Spielberg's first Best Director award
Stars Liam Neeson as Oskar Schindler, Ralph Fiennes as SS officer Amon Göth, and Ben Kingsley as Itzhak Stern
Shot almost entirely in black and white; the 'girl in the red coat' scene is one of the most memorable uses of color in film
Filmed on location in Kraków, Poland, over 72 days; Spielberg refused a salary, calling it 'blood money'
John Williams composed the score; Itzhak Perlman performed the main theme
Spielberg used his profits to establish the Shoah Foundation, which has recorded 55,000+ Holocaust survivor testimonies

items.prompt: Which 1994 film starring Tom Hanks follows a simple man from Alabama through decades of American history, from Elvis to Watergate?
items.explanation:
Directed by Robert Zemeckis; based on Winston Groom's 1986 novel
Won six Oscars: Best Picture, Best Director, Best Actor (Hanks), Best Adapted Screenplay, Best Visual Effects, Best Film Editing
Hanks won his second consecutive Best Actor Oscar (after *Philadelphia* in 1993), only the second actor to do so since Spencer Tracy
Pioneered digital compositing to insert Forrest into historical footage with JFK, Nixon, and John Lennon
'Life is like a box of chocolates' became one of the most quoted movie lines of the 1990s
The bench scenes were filmed in Savannah, Georgia's Chippewa Square
Grossed $678 million worldwide on a $55 million budget

items.prompt: Which 1997 James Cameron film about a doomed ocean liner tied *Ben-Hur*'s record with 11 Oscar wins?
items.explanation:
Directed by James Cameron; starring Leonardo DiCaprio and Kate Winslet
Won 11 Oscars from 14 nominations, tying *Ben-Hur* (1959) for most wins by a single film
First film to gross over $1 billion worldwide; at the time, the most expensive film ever made ($200 million)
Cameron shouted 'I'm the king of the world!' upon accepting Best Director — quoting his own film's most famous line
Céline Dion's 'My Heart Will Go On' won Best Original Song and became a global megahit
Cameron personally dove to the real wreck of the Titanic to shoot footage used in the film
Held the worldwide box office record for 12 years until Cameron's own *Avatar* surpassed it in 2010

items.prompt: Which 1999 film, directed by Sam Mendes in his feature debut, stars Kevin Spacey as a suburban man in midlife crisis?
items.explanation:
Directed by Sam Mendes; written by Alan Ball
Won five Oscars: Best Picture, Best Director (Mendes's debut), Best Actor (Spacey), Best Original Screenplay, Best Cinematography
Mendes was a theater director making his first film; he went on to direct *Road to Perdition* and two James Bond films
The floating plastic bag scene became an iconic (and often parodied) symbol of suburban ennui
Alan Ball later created HBO's *Six Feet Under* and *True Blood*
Grossed $356 million worldwide on a $15 million budget
Conrad L. Hall's Oscar-winning cinematography used a desaturated suburban palette with bursts of red (roses, Spacey's sports car)

items.prompt: Which 2007 Coen brothers film, based on a Cormac McCarthy novel, features Javier Bardem as the hitman Anton Chigurh?
items.explanation:
Directed by Joel and Ethan Coen; based on Cormac McCarthy's 2005 novel
Won four Oscars: Best Picture, Best Director, Best Supporting Actor (Bardem), Best Adapted Screenplay
Bardem's portrayal of Anton Chigurh and his captive-bolt pistol became one of cinema's most menacing villains
Set in 1980 West Texas; stars Josh Brolin, Tommy Lee Jones, and Bardem
The Coens shot the film with minimal score — long stretches have no music at all, only ambient sound
First Coen brothers film to win Best Picture, after previous nominations for *Fargo* (1996)
Often cited alongside *There Will Be Blood* (also 2007) as one of the best films of the 2000s

items.prompt: Which 2009 Iraq War film made Kathryn Bigelow the first woman to win the Best Director Oscar?
items.explanation:
Directed by Kathryn Bigelow; written by Mark Boal
Won six Oscars: Best Picture, Best Director, Best Original Screenplay, Best Film Editing, Best Sound Editing, Best Sound Mixing
Bigelow became the first woman to win Best Director, beating her ex-husband James Cameron (*Avatar*)
Jeremy Renner stars as a reckless U.S. Army bomb disposal technician in Baghdad
The film follows an Explosive Ordnance Disposal (EOD) team during the Iraq War in 2004
Made only $17 million at the box office — the lowest-grossing Best Picture winner in the modern era at the time
Opening title card quotes journalist Chris Hedges: 'The rush of battle is a potent and often lethal addiction'

items.prompt: Which 2013 film, directed by Steve McQueen and starring Chiwetel Ejiofor, tells the true story of a free Black man kidnapped into slavery?
items.explanation:
Directed by Steve McQueen; screenplay by John Ridley, based on Solomon Northup's 1853 memoir
Won three Oscars: Best Picture, Best Adapted Screenplay, Best Supporting Actress (Lupita Nyong'o)
First film by a Black director (McQueen) to win Best Picture
Chiwetel Ejiofor stars as Solomon Northup, a free man from New York kidnapped and sold into slavery in 1841
Michael Fassbender, Benedict Cumberbatch, and Brad Pitt (also a producer) appear in supporting roles
McQueen is a British artist and filmmaker; he won the Turner Prize in 1999 before turning to feature films
Nyong'o's debut film performance; she won the Oscar over Jennifer Lawrence and Julia Roberts
items.cardFront: 2013 — first Black director to win Best Picture

items.prompt: Which 2016 coming-of-age drama, told in three chapters, won Best Picture after *La La Land* was mistakenly announced as the winner?
items.explanation:
Directed by Barry Jenkins; based on Tarell Alvin McCraney's play *In Moonlight Black Boys Look Blue*
Won three Oscars: Best Picture, Best Supporting Actor (Mahershala Ali), Best Adapted Screenplay
The envelope mix-up: Warren Beatty was handed the wrong envelope and Faye Dunaway announced *La La Land* before the error was corrected on live television
First film with an all-Black cast and first LGBTQ-themed film to win Best Picture
Told in three chapters following Chiron from childhood to adulthood in Liberty City, Miami
Made for only $1.5 million — one of the lowest budgets for a Best Picture winner ever
Mahershala Ali became the first Muslim actor to win an Oscar

items.prompt: Which 2019 South Korean film by Bong Joon-ho became the first non-English-language film to win Best Picture?
items.explanation:
Directed by Bong Joon-ho; a South Korean black comedy thriller
Won four Oscars: Best Picture, Best Director, Best Original Screenplay, Best International Feature Film
First non-English-language film to win Best Picture in 92 years of Oscar history
Also won the Palme d'Or at Cannes — only the fourth film to win both the Palme and Best Picture
Follows the impoverished Kim family as they infiltrate a wealthy household, with a dramatic class-warfare twist
Bong's acceptance speech: 'Once you overcome the one-inch-tall barrier of subtitles, you will be introduced to so many more amazing films'
Grossed $266 million worldwide on a $11 million budget
items.cardFront: 2019 — first non-English Best Picture, Bong Joon-ho

items.prompt: Which 2020 film, directed by Chloé Zhao and starring Frances McDormand, follows a woman living as a modern-day nomad in the American West?
items.explanation:
Directed by Chloé Zhao; based on Jessica Bruder's 2017 nonfiction book
Won three Oscars: Best Picture, Best Director (Zhao), Best Actress (McDormand)
Zhao became the second woman and first woman of color to win Best Director
McDormand plays Fern, a widow who becomes a van-dwelling itinerant worker after her Nevada company town collapses
Cast real-life nomads alongside McDormand, including Linda May and Swankie playing versions of themselves
Shot on location across the American West with a small crew using natural light
Second female-directed film to win Best Picture, after Bigelow's *The Hurt Locker*

items.prompt: Which 2021 film about a hearing teenager in a deaf fishing family became the first streaming-distributed movie to win Best Picture?
items.explanation:
Directed by Siân Heder; distributed by Apple TV+
Won three Oscars: Best Picture, Best Supporting Actor (Troy Kotsur), Best Adapted Screenplay
First film distributed by a streaming service to win Best Picture — and the first Sundance premiere to do so
Troy Kotsur became the first deaf male actor to win an Oscar; Marlee Matlin (also in the cast) was the first deaf actor to win, for *Children of a Lesser God* (1986)
CODA stands for 'Child of Deaf Adults'; Emilia Jones stars as Ruby, an aspiring singer
Apple acquired distribution rights for $25 million after a Sundance bidding war in 2021
A remake of the 2014 French film *La Famille Bélier*
items.cardFront: 2021 — first streaming Best Picture, Apple TV+

items.prompt: Which 2023 Christopher Nolan film about the father of the atomic bomb won seven Oscars, including Best Picture and Best Director?
items.explanation:
Directed by Christopher Nolan; starring Cillian Murphy as J. Robert Oppenheimer
Won seven Oscars from 13 nominations: Best Picture, Best Director, Best Actor (Murphy), Best Supporting Actor (Robert Downey Jr.), Best Cinematography, Best Film Editing, Best Original Score
Nolan's first Best Director and Best Picture wins after a career spanning *Memento*, *The Dark Knight*, *Inception*, and *Interstellar*
Grossed $975 million worldwide, the highest-grossing Best Picture winner since *The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King* (2003)
Shot using IMAX cameras; some scenes used black-and-white IMAX film stock for the first time in history
Released on the same day as *Barbie*, creating the 'Barbenheimer' cultural phenomenon
Based on the biography *American Prometheus* by Kai Bird and Martin J. Sherwin
